nodes.name: reviewTiersMonthly

nodes.name: firebase-config.ts
nodes.summary: Initializes the Firebase app (guarding against double-initialization) and exports the shared `auth` instance used for phone/OTP authentication. Config values come from Vite env vars with hardcoded literals as fallback defaults.
nodes.languageNotes: The `getApps().length === 0 ? initializeApp(...) : getApp()` idiom makes the module idempotent under Vite HMR. Note the inline fallback API keys — Firebase web keys are not secrets, but the project id and sender id are still committed literals.

nodes.name: AuthContext.tsx
nodes.summary: Central React authentication context holding the current user, token, and derived role, with login/register/logout/refresh operations backed by authService and localStorage session persistence.
nodes.languageNotes: Listens for a global `unauthorized` window event to force logout when the axios interceptor sees a 401, decoupling HTTP-layer auth failures from React state.

nodes.name: roleAccess.ts
nodes.summary: Single source of truth for role-based portal routing: declares the CUSTOMER/STAFF/ADMIN login options and pure helpers mapping a user or role to its portal target and destination path.
nodes.languageNotes: LOGIN_ROLE_OPTIONS embeds seeded demo phone numbers and passwords for STAFF and ADMIN directly in shipped frontend code — these credentials reach the browser bundle.

nodes.name: RegisterForm.tsx
nodes.summary: Customer sign-up form collecting name, phone, email, and password with live pattern validation, wiring an invisible Firebase reCAPTCHA verifier before triggering the OTP registration mutation.

nodes.name: VerifyOtpForm.tsx
nodes.summary: Six-digit OTP entry component with per-digit inputs, paste handling, keyboard navigation, and a resend countdown timer; confirms the Firebase confirmationResult and completes customer registration.

nodes.name: use-auth.ts
nodes.summary: Hand-rolled mutation hooks for the Firebase phone-auth flow: sending an OTP for registration, resending an OTP, and verifying it before handing the Firebase ID token to the backend register call.
nodes.languageNotes: Pending registration payloads are stashed in sessionStorage between the send-OTP and verify-OTP steps; phone numbers are normalized from local `0…` form to `+84…` E.164 before Firebase calls.

nodes.name: LoginPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Unified marketing-and-authentication landing screen with a role selector (Customer/Staff/Admin), password login tab, and register/verify-OTP tabs, redirecting to the role's portal on success.

nodes.name: WashingCounterPage.tsx

nodes.name: ProtectedRoute.tsx
nodes.summary: Route guard component that redirects unauthenticated users to /login and users whose role is not in allowedRoles to /unauthorized.
nodes.languageNotes: Client-side only — this guard controls navigation, not data access; the backend must independently enforce the same role restrictions.

nodes.name: RootRouter.tsx
nodes.summary: Top-level BrowserRouter defining the public routes (landing, login, unauthorized) and the three role-gated portal branches: /app for CUSTOMER, /admin for ADMIN, and /counter for STAFF.

nodes.name: UnauthorizedPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Static fallback screen shown when an authenticated user's role does not permit access to the requested route.

nodes.name: auth.service.ts
nodes.summary: Axios-backed authentication API client exposing login, register, and getProfile, and normalizing backend payloads into the app's User shape with consistent error messages.

nodes.name: restoreUser
nodes.summary: Rehydrates the persisted token and user from localStorage on startup, clearing both entries if the stored JSON is unparseable.

nodes.name: AuthProvider
nodes.summary: Context provider owning auth state and exposing login, register, logout, and refreshProfile; persists sessions to localStorage and force-logs-out on the global unauthorized event.

nodes.name: useAuth
nodes.summary: Consumer hook returning the auth context value, throwing if used outside an AuthProvider.

nodes.name: getUserRole

nodes.name: canAccessAdminPortal

nodes.name: getPortalForUser
nodes.summary: Maps a user to their portal target — admin, washing, or customer — falling back to auth when no role is present.

nodes.name: getDestinationForRole
nodes.summary: Translates a role into its post-login route path: /admin, /counter, /app, or /login when unauthenticated.

nodes.name: RegisterForm
nodes.summary: Renders the registration form, validates each field against name/phone/email/password patterns, and submits through the register mutation to trigger OTP delivery.

nodes.name: VerifyOtpForm
nodes.summary: Manages the six OTP digit inputs, resend countdown, and reCAPTCHA lifecycle, verifying the code and invoking onSuccess once the account is created.

nodes.name: useCustomerRegister
nodes.summary: Mutation hook that normalizes the phone to E.164, sends a Firebase SMS OTP, and stashes the pending registration payload in sessionStorage.

nodes.name: useSendCustomerOtp
nodes.summary: Mutation hook that resends a Firebase phone OTP for an already-started registration, tracking pending and error state.

nodes.name: useVerifyCustomerOtp

nodes.name: WashingCounterPage

nodes.summary: Wizard step presenting the categorised VinaWash service menu with expandable categories and detail panels, tracking selected services and a running subtotal.

nodes.name: CustomerDashboard.tsx
nodes.summary: Customer portal dashboard showing booking history, saved vehicles, loyalty point balance and tier progress, redeemed vouchers and the point transaction log for the signed-in customer.

nodes.name: CustomerDashboard
nodes.summary: Dashboard component filtering the mock database down to the current customer and rendering bookings, vehicles, vouchers, tier progress and transaction history.

nodes.name: AdminController.java
nodes.summary: Spring REST controller exposing the admin portal API under /api/v1/admin: customer listing and update, paginated booking log, revenue reports, point audit logs, and promotion campaign CRUD. Every handler is a thin one-line delegation to AdminService.
nodes.languageNotes: Class carries only @RestController, @RequestMapping and @RequiredArgsConstructor — there is no @PreAuthorize or other method-level authorization annotation on any of the nine admin endpoints, so access control for this controller must come entirely from the global Spring Security filter chain configuration.

nodes.name: AdminController
nodes.summary: REST controller class holding nine admin endpoints that delegate to the injected AdminService. Caps the booking page size at 100 and defaults the booking date filter to today.

nodes.name: CampaignRequest.java
nodes.summary: Request DTO for creating a marketing campaign, carrying the campaign name, goal text, point multiplier, target loyalty tier, and start/end dates.

nodes.name: CampaignRequest
nodes.summary: Lombok-annotated DTO with six fields (name, goal, multiplier, targetTier, startDate, endDate) bound from the campaign creation request body and validated via @Valid at the controller boundary.

nodes.name: Promotion.java
nodes.summary: JPA entity mapping the promotions table, representing a discount campaign with its target loyalty tier, discount percentage, active date/time window, and lifecycle status.

nodes.name: Promotion
nodes.summary: Persistent promotion record with ten mapped columns including promotionId, promotionName, description, discountPercent, targetTier, start/end dates and times, and a string status used for soft deletion.

nodes.name: PromotionRepository.java
nodes.summary: Spring Data JPA repository for Promotion entities, adding derived queries that fetch promotions by status and by an active date window.

nodes.name: PromotionRepository
nodes.summary: Repository interface declaring findByStatusIgnoreCaseOrderByStartDateDesc and findByStatusIgnoreCaseAndStartDateLessThanEqualAndEndDateGreaterThanEqual for listing and date-window filtering of promotions.

nodes.name: AdminService.java
nodes.summary: Transactional service implementing all admin portal business logic: customer search and profile updates, paginated booking log, revenue aggregation by period, loyalty point audit trail, and campaign creation, listing, and soft deletion.
nodes.languageNotes: Class-level @Transactional with @Transactional(readOnly=true) on the query methods. Filtering and pagination are done in memory over repository.findAll() rather than pushed into the database, and the service performs no role check of its own — authorization is assumed to have happened upstream.

nodes.name: AdminService
nodes.summary: Admin service aggregating five repositories (customers, vehicles, bookings, point history, promotions) via constructor injection to serve the admin portal's read and write operations.

nodes.name: customers
nodes.summary: Returns all CUSTOMER-role accounts, optionally filtered by a free-text query matched against full name, phone, or any of the customer's vehicle license plates, and projects each into a map including tier, points, spend, and plates.
nodes.languageNotes: Runs a per-customer vehicle lookup inside the stream filter and again in the projection, producing an N+1 query pattern over the customer list.

nodes.name: updateCustomer
nodes.summary: Applies a partial update of a customer's name and email from a raw string map, validating email format with a regex and throwing ResourceNotFoundException or BadRequestException, then re-reads the full customer list to return the updated projection.

nodes.name: bookingLog
nodes.summary: Builds a paginated booking log by loading all bookings, filtering by optional status and date, sorting newest-created first, and slicing the requested page into a Spring PageImpl.
nodes.languageNotes: Pagination is applied in memory via subList after findAll(), so the whole booking table is materialized on every request.

nodes.name: revenue
nodes.summary: Aggregates revenue over COMPLETED bookings, grouping totals by year, month, or day depending on the period argument, and returns the grouped series alongside the overall total and completed booking count.

nodes.name: audit
nodes.summary: Returns the full loyalty point history as an audit trail sorted newest first, exposing customer id, point delta, activity type, description, and creation timestamp for each entry.

nodes.name: campaign
nodes.summary: Creates and persists a new Promotion from a CampaignRequest, rejecting an end date before the start date, generating the description tagline, uppercasing the target tier, and marking the campaign ACTIVE.

nodes.name: campaigns
nodes.summary: Read-only lookup returning every Promotion record, including ones already soft-deleted to INACTIVE status.

nodes.name: deleteCampaign
nodes.summary: Soft-deletes a campaign by loading the Promotion, throwing ResourceNotFoundException when absent, and persisting its status as INACTIVE rather than removing the row.

nodes.name: generateTagline
nodes.summary: Private helper that normalizes whitespace in a campaign goal and capitalizes it into an 'AutoWash smarter — ...' marketing tagline used as the promotion description.

nodes.name: bookingMap
nodes.summary: Private projection helper converting a Booking entity into an ordered map of admin-facing fields: id, booking reference, customer name, license plate, branch, date, time, total price, and status.

nodes.name: LoyaltyController.java
nodes.summary: REST controller exposing the loyalty API under /api/v1/loyalty: voucher redemption, per-customer voucher and point-history listings, and a maintenance endpoint that expires old points. Ownership is enforced by overwriting or ignoring client-supplied customer ids in favour of the authenticated principal.
nodes.languageNotes: Spring @RestController with Lombok @RequiredArgsConstructor; the customer identity comes from @AuthenticationPrincipal String callerId rather than the request body or path variable.

nodes.name: LoyaltyController
nodes.summary: Loyalty HTTP boundary holding a single LoyaltyService dependency and mapping four endpoints for redemption, vouchers, points, and point expiration.

nodes.name: redeem
nodes.summary: POST /vouchers/redeem handler that forcibly overwrites the request body's customerId with the authenticated caller id before delegating to LoyaltyService.redeem, returning 201 with the created voucher.
nodes.languageNotes: Overwriting r.setCustomerId(Long.valueOf(callerId)) is what prevents a caller from redeeming against another customer's point balance.

nodes.name: vouchers
nodes.summary: GET /customers/{id}/vouchers handler that ignores the {id} path variable entirely and lists vouchers for the authenticated caller instead.
nodes.languageNotes: The @PathVariable Long id is accepted but never used, so the URL is misleading even though the caller cannot read another customer's vouchers.

nodes.name: points
nodes.summary: GET /customers/{id}/points handler returning the authenticated caller's point history; like the vouchers endpoint it discards the {id} path variable.

nodes.name: expire
nodes.summary: POST /maintenance/expire-points handler that triggers the 12-month point expiration sweep and reports how many transactions were expired.
nodes.languageNotes: This mutating maintenance endpoint takes no principal and carries no method-level role annotation, so its protection depends entirely on the global Spring Security configuration.

nodes.name: RedeemVoucherRequest.java
nodes.summary: Request DTO for voucher redemption carrying customerId, voucherType, and pointsCost. The customerId field is server-overwritten by the controller, so it is not a client-trusted value.

nodes.name: RedeemVoucherRequest
nodes.summary: Mutable Lombok DTO with customerId, voucherType, and pointsCost properties used as the payload of the redeem endpoint.

nodes.name: LoyaltyService.java
nodes.summary: Transactional service implementing the loyalty domain: debiting accumulated points to issue vouchers, reading voucher and point history, and sweeping earned points older than 12 months. All point mutations are mirrored into PointHistory rows for audit.
nodes.languageNotes: Class-level @Transactional with @Transactional(readOnly = true) overrides on the query methods; voucher discount amounts are resolved through a Java 17 switch expression over the voucher type.

nodes.name: LoyaltyService
nodes.summary: Spring service coordinating CustomerRepository, VoucherRepository, and PointHistoryRepository to implement redemption, loyalty queries, and point expiration.

nodes.name: redeem
nodes.summary: Debits pointsCost from the customer's accumulated points and issues a voucher: it loads the customer, rejects insufficient balances, maps the voucher type to a fixed discount amount, saves the reduced balance, persists an ACTIVE voucher expiring in 3 months, and records a negative REDEEM point-history entry.
nodes.languageNotes: The balance check and debit run inside the class-level transaction but without optimistic locking or a re-read, so concurrent redemptions for the same customer are not explicitly guarded.

nodes.name: vouchers
nodes.summary: Read-only lookup returning a customer's vouchers ordered by expiry date descending.

nodes.name: history
nodes.summary: Read-only lookup returning a customer's point-history entries ordered by creation time descending.

nodes.name: expireOldPoints
nodes.summary: Batch sweep that finds EARN point-history rows older than 12 months, skips ones already expired via a marker in the EXPIRE description, then debits min(balance, earned points) from the customer and writes a matching negative EXPIRE history entry, returning the count processed.
nodes.languageNotes: Idempotency relies on string-matching a "#<pointHistoryId>" suffix in prior EXPIRE descriptions, and the per-row history scan inside the loop is an N+1 query pattern.

nodes.name: BookingService.java
nodes.summary: JPA join entity mapping the many-to-many relationship between bookings and wash services onto the booking_services table. Uses an embedded composite key with @MapsId so the two @ManyToOne associations share the key columns.
nodes.languageNotes: @EmbeddedId combined with @MapsId is the canonical JPA pattern for a join entity: the composite key fields are derived from the associations rather than maintained separately, avoiding duplicate booking_id/service_id state.

nodes.name: BookingService
nodes.summary: Entity for the booking_services join table, holding an embedded BookingServiceId plus @ManyToOne references to Booking and Service, each bound to a key component via @MapsId.

nodes.name: BookingServiceId.java
nodes.summary: Embeddable composite primary key for the BookingService join entity, pairing bookingId and serviceId. Implements Serializable and generates equals/hashCode via Lombok as JPA requires for id classes.
nodes.languageNotes: JPA mandates that an @Embeddable id class be Serializable and provide value-based equals/hashCode; @EqualsAndHashCode supplies the latter.

nodes.name: BookingServiceId
nodes.summary: Embeddable composite key class carrying the bookingId and serviceId columns that together identify a BookingService row.

nodes.name: BookingServiceRepository.java
nodes.summary: Spring Data JPA repository for the BookingService join entity, keyed by the composite BookingServiceId. Adds a derived query to list all service rows attached to a given booking.
nodes.languageNotes: findByBookingBookingId traverses the nested property path booking.bookingId, letting Spring Data resolve the join without a hand-written query.

nodes.name: BookingServiceRepository
nodes.summary: JpaRepository<BookingService, BookingServiceId> interface exposing CRUD plus findByBookingBookingId for fetching the services selected on one booking.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Repository guidance document for AI coding assistants working on AutoWash Pro, recording verified build facts (Java 17 / Spring Boot 3.5.6 backend, Vite frontend with only dev/build/preview scripts), a source-of-truth priority order, root-level build and test commands, and rules for workflow, verification, security, evidence logging, and review.
nodes.languageNotes: Structured as ten level-2 sections, each a short rule list rather than prose; designed to be read as machine-consumable repository policy.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Short Claude Code-specific entry document that includes AGENTS.md and PROGRESS.md by reference and adds a handful of tool-specific notes about where team rules live, which workflow tooling is authoritative, and how vendored rules and generated graphs should be treated.

nodes.name: PROGRESS.md
nodes.summary: Living project status document tracking the current milestone, verified repository facts, tooling decisions, an open checklist of unresolved credential and secret-management security blockers, the next planned actions, and the evidence policy for marking items complete.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Vietnamese-language project README describing AutoWash Pro's three role-based subsystems (customer booking and loyalty, washing counter lifecycle, admin management and reporting), the React/TypeScript, Spring Boot, and MS SQL Server tech stack, step-by-step frontend/backend/database setup instructions, and the SU26SWP08 Group 4 & 5 team credits.
nodes.languageNotes: Written in Vietnamese with emoji-prefixed headings; database setup is documented via a Docker Compose recipe rather than a manual install.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Minimal repository-root npm manifest declaring only the `docx` dependency, used for document-generation tooling rather than for building the application (the real frontend manifest lives in Front-end/package.json).

nodes.name: AutoWashPro.sql
nodes.summary: Full MSSQL schema script for the AutoWash Pro database, creating the nine core tables (customers, vehicles, bookings, services, branches, vouchers, promotions, point_history, booking_services) with primary keys, unique indexes, defaults and foreign keys, then seeding demo branches and the wash/interior service catalog.
nodes.languageNotes: SQL Server Management Studio export style: batches separated by GO, bracket-quoted identifiers, IDENTITY(1,1) surrogate keys, and idempotent IF NOT EXISTS seed blocks with SET IDENTITY_INSERT toggles.

nodes.name: customers
nodes.summary: Customer account and loyalty aggregate table holding credentials (phone, email, password_hash), role and tier, plus denormalized loyalty counters accumulated_points, total_spent and total_washes.

nodes.name: vehicles
nodes.summary: Vehicles owned by a customer, keyed by license plate and carrying vehicle_size (which drives service pricing) and an is_default flag for the booking wizard.

nodes.name: bookings
nodes.summary: Central booking record linking customer, vehicle and branch to a scheduled date/time slot, tracking status through the PENDING to COMPLETED lifecycle along with duration, total price, points earned and applied voucher or promotion.

nodes.name: services
nodes.summary: Catalog of purchasable wash and interior-detailing services with a unique service_code, base price and expected duration in minutes.

nodes.name: booking_services
nodes.summary: Join table implementing the many-to-many relation between bookings and services with a composite clustered primary key.

nodes.name: branches
nodes.summary: Physical car-wash locations with address, contact phone, daily open/close times and an ACTIVE or COMING_SOON status used to gate branch selection during booking.

nodes.name: vouchers
nodes.summary: Per-customer discount vouchers with a unique code, fixed discount amount, expiry date and redemption timestamp, referenced by bookings via applied_voucher_id.

nodes.name: promotions
nodes.summary: Admin-managed campaign definitions applying a percentage discount to a target customer tier within a date and time-of-day window.

nodes.name: point_history
nodes.summary: Append-only ledger of loyalty point movements per customer, optionally tied to a booking, with an activity_type and human-readable description.

nodes.name: FR001_FR013_upgrade_migration.sql
nodes.summary: Idempotent upgrade migration for FR-001 through FR-013 that adds the customers.role column with a CUSTOMER default, normalizes invalid role values and branch statuses, and backfills bookings.end_time and duration_minutes.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses COL_LENGTH() guards instead of IF NOT EXISTS on sys.columns so the script can be re-run safely against an already-upgraded database.

nodes.name: FR004_booking_duration_migration.sql
nodes.summary: Focused FR-004 migration adding the end_time and duration_minutes columns to bookings and backfilling existing rows with a 30-minute default duration computed via DATEADD.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite HTML entry document for the AutoWash Pro single-page app; mounts the React tree into the #root div and loads /src/main.tsx as an ES module.
nodes.languageNotes: Body element carries Tailwind utility classes including dark: variants, so the light/dark theme baseline is set in HTML rather than in CSS.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Frontend package manifest declaring the ESM React 18 + Vite application and its runtime dependencies (axios, firebase, react-router-dom, lucide-react). Exposes exactly three scripts: dev, build (tsc then vite build), and preview.
nodes.languageNotes: "type": "module" makes the whole package ESM; the build script type-checks with tsc before delegating the bundle to Vite.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the frontend: strict mode, ES2020 target, bundler module resolution, react-jsx transform, and noEmit so Vite owns emission. Entry surface is pinned to src/main.tsx and src/vite-env.d.ts.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a `files` list rather than `include`, so tsc only follows the import graph reachable from main.tsx; unreferenced modules are never type-checked. Note that `strict` is on but `noUnusedLocals`/`noUnusedParameters` are explicitly disabled.

nodes.name: skills-lock.json
nodes.summary: Lockfile pinning AI agent skill definitions sourced from the anthropics/skills GitHub repository, recording each skill's ref, path, and content hash. Developer tooling metadata only; it has no effect on the application build or runtime.

nodes.name: ERD.md
nodes.summary: Placeholder for the entity-relationship diagram of the AutoWash Pro data model; currently an empty stub containing only a heading and a TODO marker explicitly warning that it must not be cited as evidence.

nodes.name: pom.xml
nodes.summary: Maven build definition for the AutoWash Pro backend, targeting Java 17 under the Spring Boot 3.5.6 parent with web, security, validation, data-jpa, actuator, JJWT, MSSQL JDBC, Firebase Admin, springdoc-openapi, Lombok and MapStruct dependencies.
nodes.languageNotes: Lombok and MapStruct are wired together through the lombok-mapstruct-binding annotation processor path in maven-compiler-plugin, which is required for generated mappers to see Lombok accessors.

nodes.name: application.properties
nodes.summary: Spring Boot runtime configuration covering server port, MSSQL datasource, JPA/Hibernate behaviour (ddl-auto disabled, open-in-view off), JWT expiry, Firebase credential path, and VietQR payment account fields. The database password and JWT signing secret are environment-variable placeholders rather than literal values.
nodes.languageNotes: Sensitive properties are externalized via ${...} placeholders, matching the repository rule that secrets stay environment-only; the Firebase service-account JSON is still referenced from the classpath.

nodes.name: BackendApplication.java
nodes.summary: Spring Boot entry point that bootstraps the AutoWash Pro backend application context.

nodes.name: BackendApplication
nodes.summary: @SpringBootApplication class whose main method starts the embedded server and component scanning for the com.autowashpro package.

nodes.name: FirebaseConfig.java
nodes.summary: Configuration class that initializes the Firebase Admin SDK from a classpath service-account resource, used to back phone OTP authentication.

nodes.name: FirebaseConfig
nodes.summary: Spring @Configuration holding the injected Firebase credential Resource and a logger, responsible for one-time FirebaseApp initialization.

nodes.name: initFirebase
nodes.summary: @PostConstruct hook that loads the service-account stream and calls FirebaseApp.initializeApp only when no app is registered, degrading to a warning log if the credential resource is missing or unreadable.
nodes.languageNotes: Failures are swallowed into log warnings rather than rethrown, so the application still starts without Firebase credentials.

nodes.name: JwtAuthenticationFilter.java
nodes.summary: Servlet filter that extracts a Bearer token from each request, validates it, and populates the Spring Security context with the caller's id and role.

nodes.name: JwtAuthenticationFilter
nodes.summary: OncePerRequestFilter subclass holding a JwtTokenProvider dependency, applied ahead of the standard authentication filter in the security chain.

nodes.name: doFilterInternal
nodes.summary: Parses the Authorization header, validates the JWT, and sets a UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken carrying the subject and a ROLE_-prefixed authority before delegating to the rest of the chain.

nodes.name: SecurityConfig.java
nodes.summary: Central Spring Security configuration defining the stateless JWT filter chain, per-portal role-based URL rules, password encoder, and CORS policy.

nodes.name: SecurityConfig
nodes.summary: @Configuration @EnableWebSecurity class exposing the SecurityFilterChain, BCrypt password encoder, and CORS source beans for the backend.

nodes.name: securityFilterChain
nodes.summary: Builds the HTTP security chain: CSRF disabled, stateless sessions, public auth and Swagger routes, ADMIN-only admin and loyalty-maintenance routes, STAFF/ADMIN washing-counter routes, CUSTOMER vehicle and loyalty routes, and shared booking/catalog routes, with the JWT filter inserted first.
nodes.languageNotes: Role rules use hasRole/hasAnyRole, which implicitly expect the ROLE_ prefix supplied by JwtAuthenticationFilter.

nodes.name: corsConfigurationSource
nodes.summary: Registers a CORS configuration with allowed origin patterns, methods, headers, and credentials so the React frontend can call the API from a different origin.

nodes.name: BookingController.java
nodes.summary: REST controller exposing booking endpoints: branch and service catalogs, booking creation, time-slot availability, per-customer booking lists, the daily queue, and status transitions.

nodes.name: BookingController
nodes.summary: @RestController wiring BookingManagementService plus the branch and service repositories into seven compact endpoint methods, most of which are single-expression delegations.

nodes.name: availability
nodes.summary: Computes bookable time slots for a branch and date by summing selected service durations, walking the branch opening hours, and excluding slots that overlap non-cancelled existing bookings.
nodes.languageNotes: Slot generation and overlap detection live in the controller rather than the service layer, so this endpoint carries business logic that the rest of the booking flow keeps in BookingManagementService.

nodes.name: BookingResponse.java
nodes.summary: Flattened booking DTO returned to all three portals, combining customer, vehicle, branch, service, scheduling, pricing, loyalty, and VietQR payment fields.

nodes.name: BookingResponse
nodes.summary: Lombok-annotated response class with 22 denormalized fields spanning booking reference, customer and vehicle details, service names, times, total price, points earned, applied voucher, and the generated VietQR URL.

nodes.name: CreateBookingRequest.java
nodes.summary: Request payload for the booking wizard, accepting either an existing vehicle id or inline plate/brand/size details plus branch, service codes, date, time, and optional voucher.

nodes.name: CreateBookingRequest
nodes.summary: Ten-field booking creation DTO whose customerId is overwritten server-side from the authenticated caller before reaching the service layer.

nodes.name: StatusRequest.java
nodes.summary: Minimal single-field payload carrying the requested booking status for lifecycle transition endpoints.

nodes.name: SendOtpRequest.java
nodes.summary: Request DTO carrying the phone number for the OTP send step of customer authentication.

nodes.name: VehicleRequest.java
nodes.summary: Request payload for creating or updating a customer vehicle with plate, brand, size, notes, and default-vehicle flag.

nodes.name: VehicleRequest
nodes.summary: Six-field vehicle DTO whose size value feeds the size-based pricing multiplier applied during booking creation.

nodes.name: VerifyOtpRequest.java
nodes.summary: Request DTO pairing a phone number with the submitted OTP code for the verification step that issues a JWT.

nodes.name: VerifyOtpRequest
nodes.summary: Bean-validated OTP verification payload holding the phone number and code submitted by the customer portal.

nodes.name: VehicleResponse.java
nodes.summary: Response DTO returning a persisted vehicle's id, owner, plate, brand, size, notes, and default flag to the customer portal.

nodes.name: VehicleResponse
nodes.summary: Seven-field vehicle projection mirroring VehicleRequest plus the generated identifier.

nodes.name: Branch.java
nodes.summary: JPA entity for a car-wash branch, holding name, address, phone, opening and closing times, and an active/inactive status used to filter bookable locations.

nodes.name: Branch
nodes.summary: @Entity mapping the branches table; its openTime and closeTime bound the slot window computed by the availability endpoint and validated during booking creation.

nodes.name: PointHistory.java
nodes.summary: JPA entity recording each loyalty point movement, linking a customer and booking to a signed point amount, activity type, description, and timestamp.

nodes.name: PointHistory
nodes.summary: @Entity with ManyToOne associations to Customer and Booking, forming the append-only ledger behind the loyalty points balance.
nodes.languageNotes: Storing points as an immutable ledger of movements rather than a mutable balance lets expiry and audit queries run off the same table.

nodes.name: Service.java
nodes.summary: JPA entity for a purchasable wash service, carrying a lookup code, display name, description, base price, duration in minutes, and active status.

nodes.name: Service
nodes.summary: @Entity mapping the service catalog; serviceCode is the natural key used by booking requests and durationMinutes drives slot length calculation.

nodes.name: BranchRepository.java
nodes.summary: Spring Data JPA repository for Branch, adding a case-insensitive status lookup used to list active branches.

nodes.name: PointHistoryRepository.java
nodes.summary: Spring Data JPA repository for PointHistory, supporting idempotency checks on booking point awards, per-customer history listing, and expiry sweeps by activity type and age.

nodes.name: PointHistoryRepository
nodes.summary: Repository interface with three derived queries; existsByBookingBookingIdAndActivityType is what prevents double-awarding points when a booking is completed more than once.
nodes.languageNotes: Nested-property derived query names (BookingBookingId, CustomerCustomerId) traverse the ManyToOne associations without any explicit JPQL.

nodes.name: ServiceRepository.java
nodes.summary: Spring Data JPA repository for Service, exposing active-status listing and bulk lookup of services by their service codes.

nodes.name: ServiceRepository
nodes.summary: Repository interface whose findByServiceCodeIn resolves the service codes submitted by the booking wizard into priced, timed catalog rows.

nodes.name: BookingManagementService.java
nodes.summary: Core booking service owning creation, vehicle resolution, schedule validation, status transitions, completion with loyalty point awards, and DTO mapping. It coordinates eight repositories and is the hub of the PENDING to COMPLETED lifecycle.

nodes.name: BookingManagementService
nodes.summary: @Service holding booking, customer, vehicle, branch, service, booking-service, voucher, point-history and promotion repositories plus VietQR bank account properties, exposing create, customerBookings, queue and transition to the controller layer.
nodes.languageNotes: Twelve injected fields in a single service is a large surface; the class concentrates booking, pricing, voucher and loyalty concerns that would normally be split across collaborators.

nodes.name: create
nodes.summary: Creates a booking after loading the customer, rejecting a second active booking, resolving the vehicle, verifying the branch and services are ACTIVE, summing durations, and validating the requested schedule.

nodes.name: resolveVehicle
nodes.summary: Returns the customer's existing vehicle when an id is supplied, otherwise creates one from the inline plate, brand, and size fields on the request.

nodes.name: transition
nodes.summary: Applies a requested booking status change, enforcing the allowed lifecycle order and routing COMPLETED transitions through the completion side effects.

nodes.name: complete
nodes.summary: Finalizes a booking by awarding loyalty points through PointHistory, guarded so the same booking cannot be credited twice.

nodes.name: toResponse
nodes.summary: Maps a Booking entity to the flattened BookingResponse DTO, denormalizing customer, vehicle, and service fields and building the VietQR payment URL from the configured bank account.

layers.description: React entry point, root App composition, and the React Router tree (root/app/admin routers, ProtectedRoute role guard, unauthorized page) plus the customer layout chrome. This is where a browser request first lands in the SPA.

layers.name: Frontend Feature Screens
layers.description: Role-scoped screens and their view components: the customer booking wizard and dashboard, admin panels (registry, campaigns, revenue audit, tiers, vouchers), the operations/washing-counter portal, and the older pages/booking wizard variant.

layers.name: Frontend Application Logic & Data Access
layers.description: Client-side state and I/O: React contexts (auth, theme, booking), booking/pricing hooks, admin feature modules and role-access rules, the Axios instance and Firebase client, and the typed service wrappers that call the Spring API. (services/mockStore.ts also lives here but has zero inbound imports - dead code, not a live fallback.)

layers.name: Shared UI Kit, Types & Utilities
layers.description: Presentation-agnostic building blocks reused by every portal: Button/Card/Input/Modal/Stepper/Badge/Sidebar primitives, shared TypeScript domain types, formatters/validators, and app-wide constants.

layers.name: Backend REST API & DTOs
layers.description: Spring @RestController endpoints for auth, customers, vehicles, bookings, loyalty and admin, together with their request/response DTOs, MapStruct-style mappers, the custom exception hierarchy and the global exception handler that shapes error payloads.

layers.name: Backend Application Services
layers.description: Business logic for authentication/OTP, customer and vehicle management, booking management, loyalty points and vouchers, admin campaigns, and the scheduled loyalty maintenance job. Sits between the controllers and the JPA repositories.

tour.title: What AutoWash Pro Is
tour.description: Before reading a line of code, get the problem statement. README.md (Vietnamese) is the only one of these with real content: it gives the elevator pitch and the stack (React 18 + Vite front end, Spring Boot 3.5.6 / Java 17 back end, MSSQL). Be warned that docs/design/architecture.md, docs/design/ERD.md and docs/srs/SRS.md are currently 3-line TODO stubs that explicitly say "content pending - do not cite this file as evidence" - so the FR001-FR013 requirement numbers you will keep meeting in file names and migration scripts are NOT documented anywhere in this repo. The surviving record of what each FR means is the migration scripts in Back-end/database/ and the manual run-guides under docs/testing/. Ask the team for the lecturer's original rubric before assuming any FR behaviour.

tour.title: Where The Browser Lands
tour.description: main.tsx is the literal first line of application code a browser runs: it mounts React, wraps the tree in AuthProvider, and hands control to RootRouter. RootRouter is the traffic cop of the whole SPA - it decides which of the three portals (customer, admin, counter) a visitor even gets to see. Notice that routing here depends on auth state, not on the URL alone, which is why the next step is authentication rather than any screen.

tour.title: Identity And Role Routing
tour.description: AuthContext holds the logged-in user and exposes useAuth, which ProtectedRoute calls to gate every guarded route; RootRouter calls getDestinationForRole to pick the landing portal (ADMIN to /admin, STAFF to /counter, CUSTOMER to /app, no user to /login). roleAccess.ts is the single source of truth for that mapping - when role behaviour looks wrong, this file is the place to look, not the individual screens. Security note: roleAccess.ts ships seeded account credentials in source, so treat it as security-sensitive and do not copy values out of it.

tour.title: The Customer Booking Wizard
tour.description: Follow the import edge from AppRouter into BookingWizardPage, the live multi-step booking screen and the app's central user journey. Each wizard step (branch, car type, services, date/time, payment, confirmation) is its own component, while CustomerBookingContext carries the half-built booking across steps so no single step owns the whole form state. Be aware that Front-end/src/pages/booking/** holds a near-duplicate older wizard that AppRouter does not import - treat it as probable legacy code and do not edit it expecting behaviour to change.

tour.title: From Screen To HTTP Call
tour.description: useBookingWizard is the seam where UI state becomes network I/O: it imports booking.service.ts and price.service.ts and orchestrates validation, pricing and submission. Those service modules are thin typed wrappers over a single shared Axios instance in config/axios.ts, which owns the base URL and the JWT auth header - so every backend call in the app inherits its behaviour from one file. mockStore.ts is included here deliberately as a negative example: it looks like a local-data fallback, but the graph shows it has zero inbound imports - nothing in the app references it. Treat it as dead code, not as a live fallback path.

tour.title: The Shared Type Contract
tour.description: types/index.ts has by far the highest fan-in in the graph (35 inbound edges) - it is the vocabulary every screen, hook and service agrees on, and the closest thing the front end has to a schema. platform.service.ts sits just below it, mapping raw API JSON into those types via functions like mapBooking, which is where backend field names get translated into frontend ones. Read this pair before any feature work: a change here ripples further than a change almost anywhere else in the repo.

tour.title: Crossing Into Spring: The Controller
tour.description: The Axios call from Step 5 terminates here. BookingController is a thin @RestController: it validates the incoming CreateBookingRequest DTO, delegates to BookingManagementService, and returns BookingResponse - deliberately holding no business logic itself. GlobalExceptionHandler is the other half of the contract, turning the custom exception hierarchy into consistent JSON error payloads so the frontend can rely on error shape rather than parsing stack traces.

tour.title: Where The Business Rules Live
tour.description: BookingManagementService is the real brain of the booking flow. Trace its call graph: create() calls resolveVehicle() to attach or create the customer's vehicle, then toResponse() to build the DTO; transition() drives the booking status machine and calls complete(), which is where loyalty points get written as PointHistory. This is the class to read when asking 'what actually happens when a wash is booked or finished' - the controller above and the repositories below are plumbing around it.

tour.title: Entities And Repositories
tour.description: The service persists through Spring Data: BookingRepository imports the Booking entity and provides query methods with no implementation class. Study the relationships - Booking points at Customer and Vehicle, while BookingService is a join entity with a composite BookingServiceId key, because one booking can include several wash services. Customer has high fan-in (10 inbound edges) and is the hub of the domain model, so understanding its associations unlocks most of the backend.

tour.title: The Physical Schema
tour.description: The end of the request path. AutoWashPro.sql is the MSSQL schema the JPA entities map onto - the graph records defines_schema edges from these tables straight back to the entity files from Step 9, so you can check any column against its Java field. The FR-named migration scripts show how the schema grew to satisfy specific requirements from Step 1's SRS, which makes them the best surviving record of why a column exists. Read these scripts; do not execute them while exploring.

tour.title: How Requests Are Actually Authorised
tour.description: Now close the loop on Step 3: the frontend role routing is only cosmetic, and real enforcement lives here. SecurityConfig's securityFilterChain declares which endpoints are public and installs JwtAuthenticationFilter, which runs per request, extracts the bearer token that config/axios.ts attached, and validates it through JwtTokenProvider. SystemAccountSeeder creates the built-in admin and staff accounts at startup and, like roleAccess.ts, carries seeded credentials in source - flag it in any security review and never reproduce its values.

tour.title: Building, Configuring, Verifying
tour.description: Finally, the machinery around the code. pom.xml pins Java 17 and Spring Boot 3.5.6, and there is no Maven wrapper in the repo, so use an installed mvn; application.properties should hold environment-driven placeholders rather than literal secrets. On the frontend, package.json exposes exactly three scripts - dev, build, preview - and nothing else. The .mjs files under Front-end/scripts are standalone ad-hoc verification scripts run by hand: there is no wired-up test runner, no Back-end/src/test tree, and no measured coverage anywhere in this repository.
